claimText: A digital twin is a digital representation of a physical object, person, or process, contextualized in a digital version of its environment.
evidenceExcerpt: A digital twin is a digital representation of a physical object, person, or process, contextualized in a digital version of its environment.
examUse: Statement-based question defining Digital Twin technology.
distractorHooks: Confusing it with a purely static 3D model
Virtual reality without real-world physical counterparts

claimText: Digital twin technology utilizes real-time data from sensors to simulate behavior and monitor operations.
evidenceExcerpt: It uses real-time data from sensors for behavior simulation and operation monitoring.
examUse: Statement-based question on the operational mechanism of Digital Twins.
distractorHooks: Claiming it relies solely on historical data
Stating it does not use real-time sensor data

claimText: Digital twins enable organizations to simulate real situations and their outcomes to facilitate better decision-making.
evidenceExcerpt: It can help an organization simulate real situations and their outcomes, ultimately allowing it to make better decisions.
examUse: Application-based question on the utility of Digital Twins.
distractorHooks: Claiming it is only used for post-event analysis
Stating it cannot simulate future outcomes
